---
title: "Watertemp"
output: html_document
editor_options: 
  
  chunk_output_type: console
---
#test

```{r}
# ============================================================
# hydro_temp_build_from_python_nodes.R
# Purpose:
#   - Pull USGS water temp for Illinois River Basin (bbox)
#   - Map temp sites -> COMID via catchments
#   - Aggregate to temp_daily (comid, date)
#   - LEFT JOIN temp_daily onto Python-built ALL-COMID nodes parquet
#   - Write hydro_temp_data_analysis_pre2013 + hydro_temp_data_trainval_2013plus
# ============================================================


# -------------------------
# 0) Shared paths / helpers
# -------------------------
source("SRC/Functions_Paths.R")

# You must have these in Functions_Paths.R OR set them here:
#   nhd_dir, daily_drivers_dir
#   temp_clean_out, temp_comid_out
#   (and your download_temp() function, if you used one)
#
# Python outputs (set to your real paths):
nodes_parquet_dir <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "hydro_nodes_allcomids_parquet")   # directory
edges_parquet     <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "edge_list.parquet")              # file

flowline_out   <- file.path(nhd_dir, "flowlines.gpkg")
catchment_out  <- file.path(nhd_dir, "catchments.gpkg")

# Output hydro+temp files
out_analysis <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "hydro_temp_data_analysis_pre2013.parquet")
out_trainval <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "hydro_temp_data_trainval_2013plus.parquet")

# Time window
start_date <- as.Date("1984-01-01")
end_date   <- as.Date("2025-09-10")
cut_date   <- as.Date("2013-01-01")

# -------------------------
# 1) Illinois River Basin bbox (HUC 0712 approx)
# -------------------------
bbox_illinois <- c(-93.5, 36.8, -87.5, 42.0)  # West, South, East, North

# -------------------------
# 2) Water temperature metadata (00010)
# -------------------------
meta_temp <- read_waterdata_ts_meta(
  parameter_code = "00010",
  bbox = bbox_illinois,
  properties = c("monitoring_location_id", "parameter_code", "begin", "end", "time_series_id"),
  skipGeometry = TRUE
)

meta_temp_clean <- meta_temp %>%
  filter(!is.na(begin), !is.na(end)) %>%
  mutate(begin = as.Date(begin), end = as.Date(end)) %>%
  group_by(monitoring_location_id) %>%
  summarise(
    parameter = first(parameter_code),
    start_date = min(begin),
    end_date = max(end),
    n_series = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

write.csv(meta_temp_clean, "illinois_water_temp_metadata.csv", row.names = FALSE)

sites_df <- meta_temp_clean %>% distinct(monitoring_location_id)

# -------------------------
# 3) Download temperature for all sites
#     IMPORTANT: assumes you already have a function:
#       download_temp(site_id) -> returns a data.frame with columns:
#         monitoring_location_id, Date, <one or more *Wtemp columns>
#     If you don’t, keep your existing download_temp() definition in SRC.
# -------------------------
# Example expectation:
# download_temp <- function(site_id) { ... }

all_temp_data <- purrr::map_dfr(sites_df$monitoring_location_id, download_temp)

# -------------------------
# 4) Clean + aggregate temp to one value per (site, Date)
# -------------------------
temp_cols <- grep("Wtemp$", names(all_temp_data), value = TRUE)

all_temp_clean <- all_temp_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(temp_cols),
    names_to = "sensor",
    values_to = "temp_value"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(temp_value)) %>%
  group_by(monitoring_location_id, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    water_temp_C = mean(temp_value, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

if (!exists("temp_clean_out")) {
  temp_clean_out <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "illinois_water_temp_clean.csv")
}

write.csv(all_temp_clean, temp_clean_out, row.names = FALSE)

# -------------------------
# 5) Get site coordinates + map sites -> COMID
# -------------------------
site_ids_clean <- gsub("^USGS-", "", unique(all_temp_clean$monitoring_location_id))

site_info <- read_waterdata_monitoring_location(
  monitoring_location_id = paste0("USGS-", site_ids_clean),
  skipGeometry = FALSE
)

site_coords <- site_info %>%
  filter(!is.na(geometry)) %>%
  st_as_sf() %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  mutate(
    longitude = st_coordinates(.)[, 1],
    latitude  = st_coordinates(.)[, 2]
  )

catchments <- st_read(catchment_out, quiet = TRUE) %>%
  st_transform(5070)

site_coords_5070 <- st_transform(site_coords, 5070)

sites_with_comid <- st_join(
  site_coords_5070,
  catchments %>% select(comid),
  join = st_nearest_feature
) %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  distinct(monitoring_location_id, comid)

# -------------------------
# 6) Add COMID to temp + aggregate to (comid, date)
# -------------------------
all_temp_comid <- all_temp_clean %>%
  left_join(sites_with_comid, by = "monitoring_location_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(comid))

write.csv(all_temp_comid, temp_comid_out, row.names = FALSE)

temp_daily <- all_temp_comid %>%
  mutate(
    comid = as.integer(comid),
    date  = as.Date(Date)
  ) %>%
  filter(date >= start_date, date <= end_date) %>%
  group_by(comid, date) %>%
  summarise(
    water_temp_C = ifelse(all(is.na(water_temp_C)), NA_real_, mean(water_temp_C, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# Hard uniqueness check (cheap, in-memory: temp is small)
if (any(duplicated(temp_daily[c("comid", "date")]))) {
  stop("temp_daily has duplicate (comid,date) rows — fix aggregation.")
}

cat("\nTemp daily summary:\n")
print(temp_daily %>% summarise(n_rows=n(), n_comids=n_distinct(comid), min_date=min(date), max_date=max(date)))

# -------------------------
# 7) Open Python-built NODES (ALL COMIDs) with Arrow + join temp
# -------------------------
# Python wrote: hydro_nodes_allcomids_parquet/nodes.parquet (in your run)
# Using open_dataset on the directory works for a single parquet file too.
nodes_ds <- arrow::open_dataset(nodes_parquet_dir, format = "parquet")

sch <- nodes_ds$schema
print(sch$names)
# Quick sanity without pulling 119M rows into RAM
cat("\nNodes (Arrow) columns:\n")
print(names(nodes_ds))

# Build Arrow table for temp_daily for joining
temp_tbl <- arrow::arrow_table(temp_daily)

# LEFT JOIN in Arrow (lazy)
nodes_with_temp <- nodes_ds %>%
  left_join(temp_tbl, by = c("comid", "date"))

# Optional: spot-check counts (still computed via Arrow)
nodes_stats <- nodes_with_temp %>%
  summarise(
    n_rows  = n(),
    n_comid = n_distinct(comid),
    min_date = min(date),
    max_date = max(date),
    pct_rows_with_temp = mean(!is.na(water_temp_C))
  ) %>%
  collect()

cat("\nNODES+TEMP summary (Arrow computed):\n")
print(nodes_stats)

# -------------------------
# 8) Split + write parquet outputs (still Arrow -> disk, no RAM blowup)
# -------------------------
analysis_ds <- nodes_with_temp %>% filter(date < cut_date)
trainval_ds <- nodes_with_temp %>% filter(date >= cut_date)

# Write to single parquet files (fast enough, but big).
# If you prefer partitioned datasets, I can switch this to write_dataset().
arrow::write_parquet(analysis_ds, out_analysis, compression = "zstd")
arrow::write_parquet(trainval_ds, out_trainval, compression = "zstd")

cat("\nWrote:\n")
cat(" - ", out_analysis, "\n", sep = "")
cat(" - ", out_trainval, "\n", sep = "")

# -------------------------
# 9) Flowlines COMID count (your last chunk)
# -------------------------
flowlines <- st_read(flowline_out, quiet = TRUE) %>% mutate(comid = as.integer(comid))
cat("\nUnique flowlines COMIDs:", dplyr::n_distinct(flowlines$comid), "\n")

# Edges exist already (Python wrote them); just confirm:
if (file.exists(edges_parquet)) {
  edge_meta <- arrow::read_parquet(edges_parquet) %>%
    summarise(n_rows=n(), n_comids=n_distinct(comid))
  cat("\nEdges parquet summary:\n")
  print(edge_meta)
} else {
  warning("Edges parquet not found at: ", edges_parquet)
}

```


#end test
```{r setup, message=TRUE, warning=TRUE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)

# Shared paths, packages, helper functions
source("SRC/Functions_Paths.R")
```

```{r}

#  Illinois River Basin bounding box

# Approximate bounding box for HUC 0712 (Illinois River Basin)
# Format: c(West, South, East, North)
bbox_illinois <- c(-93.5, 36.8, -87.5, 42.0)  

# Water temperature metadata

meta_temp <- read_waterdata_ts_meta(
  parameter_code = "00010",   # Water Temperature (°C)
  bbox = bbox_illinois,       # Limit to Illinois River Basin
  properties = c("monitoring_location_id", "parameter_code", "begin", "end", "time_series_id"),
  skipGeometry = TRUE
)

# Clean and summarize metadata
meta_temp_clean <- meta_temp %>%
  filter(!is.na(begin), !is.na(end)) %>%
  mutate(begin = as.Date(begin), end = as.Date(end)) %>%
  group_by(monitoring_location_id) %>%
  summarise(
    parameter = first(parameter_code),
    start_date = min(begin),
    end_date = max(end),
    n_series = n(),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

# sanity check
head(meta_temp_clean)

# save metadata
write.csv(meta_temp_clean, "illinois_water_temp_metadata.csv", row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}
# pull sites for water temp 
sites_df <- meta_temp_clean %>%
  distinct(monitoring_location_id)

# Time window of interest
start_date <- as.Date("1984-01-01")
end_date   <- as.Date("2025-09-10")


```

```{r}
# loop over all sites
all_temp_data <- map_dfr(sites_df$monitoring_location_id, download_temp)

```

```{r}

# Identify all water temp columns (exclude the *_cd flag columns)
temp_cols <- grep("Wtemp$", names(all_temp_data), value = TRUE)

# Pivot longer so each temp sensor reading is stacked
all_temp_long <- all_temp_data %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = all_of(temp_cols),
    names_to = "sensor",
    values_to = "temp_value"
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(temp_value))  # keep only valid readings

# Aggregate to one value per site/date (mean of sensors)
all_temp_clean <- all_temp_long %>%
  group_by(monitoring_location_id, Date) %>%
  summarise(
    water_temp_C = mean(temp_value, na.rm = TRUE),  # or median() if preferred
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#sanity check
head(all_temp_clean)

write.csv(all_temp_clean, temp_clean_out, row.names = FALSE)



```

```{r}
# Clean site IDs: remove "USGS-" prefix for NWIS calls
site_ids_clean <- gsub("^USGS-", "", unique(all_temp_clean$monitoring_location_id))

# Query site metadata (geometry auto-included if skipGeometry = FALSE)
site_info <- read_waterdata_monitoring_location(
  monitoring_location_id = paste0("USGS-", site_ids_clean),
  skipGeometry = FALSE   # Keep geometry for coordinates
)

#  Extract coordinates from geometry
site_coords <- site_info %>%
  filter(!is.na(geometry)) %>%
  st_as_sf() %>%
  st_transform(4326) %>%
  mutate(
    longitude = st_coordinates(.)[,1],
    latitude  = st_coordinates(.)[,2]
  )

# Load catchments (must already be in your nhd_dir folder)
catchments <- st_read(file.path(nhd_dir, "catchments.gpkg"), quiet = TRUE)

# Make sure everything is in the same CRS (we use EPSG:5070 for NHDPlus)
catchments <- st_transform(catchments, 5070)  

# Reproject site points to EPSG:5070 to match NHDPlus
site_coords_5070 <- st_transform(site_coords, 5070)

```

```{r}
# Spatial join: assign each site to its catchment
sites_with_comid <- st_join(
  site_coords_5070,
  catchments %>% select(comid),  # keep only COMID column
  join = st_nearest_feature
)

# Keep unique mapping of site to COMID
sites_with_comid <- sites_with_comid %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%   # drop geometry for final table
  distinct(monitoring_location_id, comid)

# sanity check
head(sites_with_comid)

```

```{r}
# Add COMID to water temperature dataset
all_temp_comid <- all_temp_clean %>%
  left_join(sites_with_comid, by = "monitoring_location_id") %>%
  filter(!is.na(comid))   # keep only sites with COMID assigned

# Check
head(all_temp_comid)

# Save for later use in model
write.csv(all_temp_comid, temp_comid_out, row.names = FALSE)

```

```{r}

# Build hydro_temp_data with strict (comid, date) uniqueness, then split 
# Build hydro_temp_data with strict (comid, date) uniqueness, then split 
# Inputs
out_path_nodes <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "hydro_data.parquet")
out_path_edges <- file.path(daily_drivers_dir, "edge_list.parquet")  # kept for parity (not used here)

hydro_data <- read_parquet(out_path_nodes) %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date))

# 1) Enforce one row per (comid, date) in hydro_data (dedup safely)
hydro_data_dedup <- hydro_data %>%
  group_by(comid, date) %>%
  summarise(
    across(where(is.numeric), ~ ifelse(all(is.na(.x)), NA_real_, mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE))),
    across(where(~ !is.numeric(.x)), ~ dplyr::first(.x)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

dup_h <- sum(duplicated(hydro_data_dedup[c("comid", "date")]))
if (dup_h > 0) stop(paste("hydro_data still has duplicate (comid,date) after dedup:", dup_h))

# 2) Aggregate temperature to one row per (comid, date) (robust to all-NA days)
temp_daily <- all_temp_comid %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(Date)) %>%
  group_by(comid, date) %>%
  summarise(
    water_temp_C = ifelse(all(is.na(water_temp_C)), NA_real_, mean(water_temp_C, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

dup_t <- sum(duplicated(temp_daily[c("comid", "date")]))
if (dup_t > 0) stop(paste("temp_daily has duplicate (comid,date):", dup_t))

# 3) Join (should remain unique)
hydro_temp_data <- hydro_data_dedup %>%
  left_join(temp_daily, by = c("comid", "date"))

dup_j <- sum(duplicated(hydro_temp_data[c("comid", "date")]))
if (dup_j > 0) stop(paste("hydro_temp_data has duplicate (comid,date) after join:", dup_j))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# 4) REMOVE SENTINELS BEFORE SPLIT/WRITE  (-9998 -> 0)
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
sentinel_mode <- "threshold"  # "exact" or "threshold"
sentinel_val  <- -9998
sentinel_thr  <- -9990        # used only if mode == "threshold"

# quick diagnostic: count sentinel-ish values per numeric column (before)
sentinel_counts_before <- hydro_temp_data %>%
  summarise(across(
    where(is.numeric),
    ~ if (sentinel_mode == "exact") sum(.x == sentinel_val, na.rm = TRUE) else sum(.x <= sentinel_thr, na.rm = TRUE)
  ))

cat("\n=== Sentinel counts BEFORE replacement (numeric cols) ===\n")
print(sentinel_counts_before %>% tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "col", values_to = "n_bad") %>%
        filter(n_bad > 0) %>% arrange(desc(n_bad)))

# replace sentinels -> 0
hydro_temp_data <- hydro_temp_data %>%
  mutate(across(
    where(is.numeric),
    ~ if (sentinel_mode == "exact") {
        dplyr::if_else(.x == sentinel_val, 0, .x)
      } else {
        dplyr::if_else(.x <= sentinel_thr, 0, .x)
      }
  ))

# quick diagnostic: count sentinel-ish values per numeric column (after)
sentinel_counts_after <- hydro_temp_data %>%
  summarise(across(
    where(is.numeric),
    ~ if (sentinel_mode == "exact") sum(.x == sentinel_val, na.rm = TRUE) else sum(.x <= sentinel_thr, na.rm = TRUE)
  ))

cat("\n=== Sentinel counts AFTER replacement (numeric cols) ===\n")
print(sentinel_counts_after %>% tidyr::pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "col", values_to = "n_bad") %>%
        filter(n_bad > 0) %>% arrange(desc(n_bad)))

# --------------------------------------------------------------------
# 5) Split + write
# --------------------------------------------------------------------
cut_date <- as.Date("2013-01-01")

hydro_temp_analysis <- hydro_temp_data %>% filter(date < cut_date)
hydro_temp_trainval <- hydro_temp_data %>% filter(date >= cut_date)

write_parquet(
  hydro_temp_analysis,
  sub("\\.parquet$", "_analysis_pre2013.parquet", out_path_nodes_temp)
)

write_parquet(
  hydro_temp_trainval,
  sub("\\.parquet$", "_trainval_2013plus.parquet", out_path_nodes_temp)
)

cat("\nWrote:\n",
    " - ", sub("\\.parquet$", "_analysis_pre2013.parquet", out_path_nodes_temp), "\n",
    " - ", sub("\\.parquet$", "_trainval_2013plus.parquet", out_path_nodes_temp), "\n", sep="")


```


```{r}
n_distinct(flowlines$comid)
```
